title: Guest Complaint Resolution Playbook for Hotels in Egypt
trigger: A guest lodges a complaint at the front desk or through any hotel communication channel.

steps.title: Acknowledge the Complaint
steps.action: The front desk agent or concierge should immediately acknowledge the guest's complaint with empathy and understanding. Use phrases like 'I understand how this could be frustrating for you' to validate their feelings.

steps.title: Gather Details
steps.action: Politely ask the guest for more information regarding their complaint. Document the details in the PMS and ensure all relevant information is captured, including time, location, and nature of the issue.

steps.title: Evaluate the Complaint
steps.action: Assess the severity of the complaint. Determine if it can be resolved immediately or if it requires escalation. Use the hotel's SOPs to guide this evaluation.

next_steps.condition: Complaint is minor and can be resolved immediately
Complaint is severe or requires management intervention

steps.title: Resolve the Complaint
steps.action: If the complaint can be resolved immediately, take the necessary actions to address the issue. Offer a small token of apology, such as a complimentary service or discount, if appropriate.

steps.title: Escalate the Complaint
steps.action: If the complaint is severe or cannot be resolved at the front desk, escalate it to the Duty Manager. Provide all documented details and any actions taken so far.

steps.title: Follow-up with the Guest
steps.action: After resolution or escalation, follow up with the guest to ensure their satisfaction with the outcome. Document the follow-up in the PMS and note any additional feedback.

description: This playbook provides a structured approach for hotel staff in Egypt to handle guest complaints effectively, ensuring guest satisfaction and maintaining high service standards.
escalation_path: Front Desk Supervisor -> Duty Manager -> General Manager
